questions.scenario: The team needs regional HTTP routing for `orders.devpolaris.com`, TLS termination, path-based rules, and backend health probes. Which service fits best?
questions.explanation: Application Gateway is the regional layer 7 option for HTTP routing, TLS termination, and backend health checks.

questions.scenario: The team needs a global edge entry for users in multiple regions and wants traffic routed to the closest healthy origin. Which service fits best?
questions.explanation: Front Door is the global HTTP edge entry pattern. Private endpoints, route tables, and identities solve different problems.

questions.scenario: Which statements are true about health probes?

options.text: They help keep unhealthy backends out of rotation.
They prove every user action works.
They should hit a path that reflects whether the backend can serve traffic.
They can fail even when the VM or container process is running.

questions.explanation: Health probes are operating signals, not full end-to-end tests. A running process can still fail the probe path.
